title: FORTIS Phase 1: Chassis Feasibility & Arm Stability
conclusion: SKID-STEER ON THE 4.5" STEP IS FEASIBLE

wheel.note: Wheel diameter does not affect static stability

vertical_reach.status: STABLE - good margin for all vertical poses
vertical_reach.note: Primary pose for reaching high surfaces up to 55" from floor

scorpion_reach.operational_limit: Maintain shoulder pitch >= 60 deg in scorpion pose

lateral_reach.status: UNSTABLE at all tested shoulder angles when j0=90deg
lateral_reach.operational_constraint: Pure lateral reach (arm perpendicular to chassis) is not safe at any shoulder pitch with this arm length. Operator must avoid pure 90-degree lateral deployment. Combine lateral reach with some forward/backward component, or drive closer to the target.
lateral_reach.note: Margins are very close to zero (-0.008 at 60 deg shoulder). Cable tension from the lowering system provides additional safety margin not modeled in static analysis.

operational_constraints: No pure lateral reach (j0=90/270 deg) - drive closer instead
Scorpion pose: maintain shoulder pitch >= 60 degrees
Vertical reach: unrestricted, stable at all tested configurations
Stowed: unrestricted, stable with large margin
Cable from lowering system provides unmeasured safety margin

skid_steer_feasibility.note: Weight and friction only affect torque, not pass/fail or stability

parameter_sensitivity_summary.track_width: Most important. Wider = more stable. 13" optimal (14.5" with wheels fits tunnel)
parameter_sensitivity_summary.arm_length: Shorter = more stable, but must meet 55" reach. 46" is minimum viable.
parameter_sensitivity_summary.chassis_height: Taller = higher arm base = shorter arm needed. H=9" best with 46" arm.
parameter_sensitivity_summary.weight: Heavier = more stable (dilutes arm COG). 50 lbs recommended.
parameter_sensitivity_summary.mount_type: Top mount slightly better than recessed when arm is long (raises base height).
parameter_sensitivity_summary.friction: Does not affect stability. Only affects scrub torque magnitude.
